chapter_title.ar: باب التَّفْرِقَةِ بَيْنَ ذَوِي الْأَرْحَامِ مِنَ الْمَمَالِيكِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 96 – The separation between the relatives from the owned slaves

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/252)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/252)
text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ أَخَوَيْنِ مَمْلُوكَيْنِ هَلْ يُفَرَّقُ بَيْنَهُمَا وَ عَنِ الْمَرْأَةِ وَ وَلَدِهَا قَالَ لَا هُوَ حَرَامٌ إِلَّا أَنْ يُرِيدُوا ذَلِكَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Usman Bin Isa, from Sama’at who said,

‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about two brothers who are owned slaves, is effecting a separation between the two Permissible, and about the woman and her child?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘No, it is Prohibited, except if they want that’.
en.sarwar: 2. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ‘Uthman ibn ‘Isa from Sama‘ah who has said the following: “I once asked him (the Imam), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of two slaves who are brothers; if they can be separated from each other or a woman slave, if she can be separated from her children. He (the Imam) said, ‘No, it is not lawful unless they want it.’”
